manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: port, teacher, band, swing, crash, phoenix, plate, berry, pirate.
Your opponent's team words are: contract, capital, face, green, telescope, oil, root, kiwi.
Distractor words are: heart, plastic, war, pan, ninja, millionaire, snowman, orange.
The kill words are: .

message.reasoning:
We need to give a one-word clue linking some of our team's words. Must not be any of the listed words or contain parts. List of our team words: port, teacher, band, swing, crash (maybe crash), phoenix, plate, berry, pirate.

Opponent: contract, capital, face, green, telescope, oil, root, kiwi.

Distractors: heart, plastic, war, pan, ninja, millionaire, snowman, orange.

Kill words: none? It says kill words are: . maybe none.

We need to give clue and list which target words we intend to be guessed. Choose a clue that links multiple. For example "music" could link band, swing (both music styles), maybe pirate (sea shanties). But "music" is not in the list. Could also link "port" (port as harbor, ???). Maybe "school" for teacher, band (school band), swing (school playground swing), but not others. Could choose "sea" linking port, pirate, crash (ship crash), maybe berry? not.

Maybe "fruit" links berry, maybe also "port" (port wine made from grapes). Not strong.

Could use "jam" linking band (jam session), berry (jam), maybe swing (dance)? Could be.

Clue: "jam". That links band (jam session), berry (jam fruit), maybe swing (dance jam?). Also "crash"? No.

Or clue "concert" links band, swing (genre), teacher? maybe not. "pirate" could link "booty". Booty relates to pirate, berry (booty berry?), not.

Maybe "swing" as a sport? Not.

Clue "nectar" link berry, maybe phoenix? no.

Clue "sail" could link port, pirate, band? no.

Clue "class" could link teacher, band (class band), maybe swing (class swing?), not.

Clue "studio" maybe teacher, band.

Better to aim for 2-3 words. For safety, choose a clue that strongly links two words, avoiding risk of opponent words.

Band and swing both are music genres. Clue "jazz" connects swing (jazz swing) and band (jazz band). Also maybe teacher? not. Could also connect "pirate"? no. "jazz" not an opponent word.

But ensure not containing parts of opponent words: "jazz" is fine.

Thus: CLUE: Jazz
TARGETS: band, swing

Make sure format: CLUE: Jazz
TARGETS: band, swing

Yes
